valid_core: Stage5 substrate utilities after narrowing responsibilities

task_groups.title: Phase A. Cleanup / Reset to Valid Core
Phase B. PRD Implementation on Clean Baseline
Phase C. Post-Implementation Cleanup and Authority Hardening

tasks.description: Define the reset boundary and valid-core inventory from origin/master. Explicitly mark current Stage6/web/tgbot as legacy and lock the retained baseline to reusable substrate and runtime foundations only.
acceptance_criteria: A reset-boundary note exists and maps keep, keep with refactor, quarantine/disable, and delete/rewrite areas across src/, deploy/, and docs/planning/.
The retained baseline contains only code that is code-backed, minimally necessary, PRD-compatible, and reusable without current Stage6/web/tgbot semantics.
The note explicitly states that current Stage6/web/tgbot are not the target architecture.
test_steps: Review the note against the PRD, AGENTS.md, Program.cs, Stage6/, Web/, Telegram/Bot/, TgAssistant.Mcp/index.ts, and docker-compose.yml.
Verify that every retained item can survive without legacy Stage6/web/tgbot behavior.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln after any code changes.
tasks.notes: This is the cleanup gate that prevents legacy carry-forward by inertia.

tasks.description: Deprecate planning and runbook documents that normalize the accepted Stage6/web/tgbot baseline, and publish a clean-slate planning index anchored to the PRD and reset boundary.
acceptance_criteria: docs/planning/README.md points to the clean-slate pack instead of N1 or Stage6/web readiness docs.
Legacy Stage6/web planning files are marked deprecated or archival with an explicit reason.
No active planning doc claims that current Stage6/web/tgbot is the implementation path.
test_steps: Search docs/planning/ and docs/runbooks/ for 'Stage 6 accepted', 'readiness baseline', and 'web delivery' and confirm active docs redirect to the clean plan.
Open the new planning index and verify that the PRD and reset-boundary note are the active authority.
If doc paths change, run any available markdown or link validation used in the repo.
tasks.notes: Historical docs may remain, but they must stop driving execution.

tasks.description: Shrink the shared model and interface surface to a valid baseline. Remove or isolate Stage6/current-state/profile/strategy/draft/review/case/user-context contracts from active core namespaces.
acceptance_criteria: Stage6-specific models and interfaces are no longer presented as active foundation contracts in TgAssistant.Core.
Retained shared models are limited to substrate, ingest, archive/media, evidence capture, queue, and minimal operational primitives.
Any preserved legacy contracts are clearly marked deprecated or moved behind a legacy boundary.
test_steps: Inspect src/TgAssistant.Core/Models/ and src/TgAssistant.Core/Interfaces/ and confirm legacy Stage6 contracts are removed, relocated, or marked legacy-only.
Run rg to verify that the active composition root no longer depends on Stage6 contracts as baseline APIs.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Current Core over-encodes the rejected Stage6 product shape. Completed by moving legacy Stage6/current-state/profile/strategy/draft/review/case contracts behind TgAssistant.Core.Legacy namespaces and splitting legacy repositories out of the active core interface surface.

tasks.description: Reset the schema and repository baseline. Separate preserved substrate tables from frozen legacy Stage6/domain product tables so new PRD work does not inherit old truth storage.
acceptance_criteria: A schema inventory labels each table and repository as retained substrate, retained with refactor, frozen legacy, or replace-on-rebuild.
Stage6 case/artifact/profile/strategy/draft/review repositories stop presenting frozen tables as active architecture.
Migration policy for the new PRD schema is defined without treating legacy Stage6 tables as canonical truth.
test_steps: Inspect TgAssistantDbContext, repository registrations, and migration files to confirm preserved versus frozen areas are explicit.
Verify the schema inventory names replacement boundaries for Stage6 tables and repos.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln after code or migration manifest changes.
tasks.notes: This task defines what survives from origin/master at the storage layer. Completed by inventorying retained/frozen table families, splitting active versus legacy repository registrations, and explicitly marking frozen domain/Stage6 mappings as non-canonical in TgAssistantDbContext and planning notes.

tasks.description: Unwind legacy runtime wiring so Program.cs, hosted-service registration, runtime roles, and smokes no longer assume current Stage6/web/tgbot as normal runtime baseline.
acceptance_criteria: The cleaned host composition separates preserved substrate/runtime roles from disabled or legacy-only roles.
Current Stage6/web/bot hosted services are not part of the active baseline runtime role contract.
Runtime docs and smokes distinguish preserved substrate verification from legacy-only diagnostics.
test_steps: Inspect Program.cs and Host/Startup/*.cs and verify Stage6/web/bot are not default baseline roles.
Run dotnet run --project src/TgAssistant.Host/TgAssistant.Host.csproj -- --list-smokes and confirm cleaned role and smoke naming.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Host wiring is one of the main places where legacy assumptions remain normalized. Completed by separating preserved verification entrypoints from legacy diagnostics in Program.cs help text, renaming launch verification step labels away from web-prefixed names, and marking retired Stage6 runtime docs as legacy-only.

tasks.description: Quarantine the current Stage6 implementation and helper commands so it cannot be mistaken for the new PRD Stage 6/7/8 path.
acceptance_criteria: Current Stage6 services, verifiers, and helper commands are removed from active runtime composition or placed behind an explicit legacy gate.
No active module or planning doc refers to current Stage6 as the baseline for bootstrap, durable formation, or crystallization.
Any retained Stage6 code is marked as legacy reference only.
test_steps: Search the codebase for active TgAssistant.Intelligence.Stage6 registrations/usages from Host startup and confirm they are gated or removed.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
Verify that no task in Phase B depends on preserving old Stage6 semantics.
tasks.notes: This is isolation, not refinement. Legacy Stage6 code is labeled as legacy-only and remains for reference/diagnostics only.

tasks.description: Disable or quarantine the current web HTML renderer and Telegram bot operator workflow. Leave at most a clearly marked temporary diagnostic shell until the new person-first surfaces exist.
acceptance_criteria: Current web and tgbot surfaces are no longer described or exposed as the target operator workflow.
If a temporary shell remains, it is labelled diagnostic-only and not presented as product baseline.
Legacy Stage6 queue/case/artifact flows are isolated from the clean implementation track.
test_steps: Inspect src/TgAssistant.Web/, WebRuntimeHostedService.cs, and TelegramBotHostedService.cs and confirm the legacy operator surface is disabled, gated, or clearly marked.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
If a temporary surface remains, open it locally and confirm it advertises itself as legacy or diagnostic-only.
tasks.notes: The current web layer is an internal renderer, not a reusable operator foundation. Legacy web and bot shell touchpoints are labeled diagnostic-only wherever they remain reachable.

tasks.description: Strip Stage6-specific MCP tools, compose defaults, and deploy assumptions so MCP and deployment carry only reusable runtime responsibilities while the clean architecture is rebuilt.
acceptance_criteria: The MCP surface no longer presents Stage6 artifact/case-centric tools as the primary contract.
docker-compose.yml and deploy docs stop assuming web/bot/Stage6 operator flow as accepted baseline runtime.
Preserved MCP/deploy shell remains usable for health, transport, and future clean-model exposure.
test_steps: Inspect src/TgAssistant.Mcp/index.ts, docker-compose.yml, and deploy/ docs to confirm Stage6-centric defaults are removed or legacy-gated.
Run npm --prefix src/TgAssistant.Mcp run build if MCP code changes.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln if Host or shared contracts change.
tasks.notes: MCP transport/auth shell is reusable; its current tool catalog is not. Default docker-compose and deploy docs now reflect only the preserved shell baseline, while legacy web/bot/Stage6 paths are marked diagnostic-only or preview-only.

tasks.description: Audit the cleanup branch for hidden legacy bias. Cleanup and implementation must stay separate, the retained baseline must be minimal and code-backed, and no reset task may read as 'finish old Stage6/web/tgbot'.
acceptance_criteria: A cleanup review note exists with findings or explicit approval across docs, models, DB, runtime, Stage6 isolation, web/tgbot, and MCP/deploy.
Any remaining dependency on legacy Stage6/web/tgbot is called out as blocking unless it is explicitly temporary and diagnostic-only.
Phase B does not start until cleanup approval is recorded or blocking findings are resolved.
test_steps: Review cleanup changes against the PRD, reset-boundary note, and this backlog.
Rerun the required build commands for touched C# and TypeScript areas.
Confirm no implementation task depends on preserving old Stage6 case/artifact semantics.
tasks.notes: This is the explicit gate against legacy carry-forward. Approval granted after CLEANUP-009-B removed active Stage6 verification dependencies from the preserved baseline and narrowed launch readiness to foundation, stage5, and budget-visibility.

tasks.description: Publish the clean-slate target architecture on top of the cleaned baseline: preserved substrate, person-first knowledge core, Stage 6 bootstrap, Stage 7 durable formation, Stage 8 crystallizers, and rollout cut lines.
acceptance_criteria: A target-architecture note maps PRD terms to concrete modules, storage boundaries, and runtime flows.
The architecture does not depend on legacy Stage6 case/artifact/profile/strategy/draft/review storage or operator flows.
Workstreams and dependency order are explicit enough to drive implementation without assistant-first drift.
test_steps: Review the architecture note against the PRD and approved cleanup outputs.
Verify that every planned module has a clean baseline dependency and no hidden reliance on current Stage6/web/tgbot.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln if project boundaries or code change.
tasks.notes: This becomes the implementation authority for Phase B. Target architecture note and workstream/dependency map are complete.

tasks.description: Introduce the new PRD data platform: Person, OperatorModel, SourceObject, EvidenceItem, RelationshipEdge, candidate identity state, ModelPassRun, NormalizationRun, and durable-object metadata.
acceptance_criteria: New person-first schema objects exist and do not reuse frozen Stage6 truth tables as canonical storage.
Identity, evidence, run audit, and durable-object metadata are queryable by person scope, evidence ref, and run id.
The schema supports later bootstrap, durable formation, clarification, and crystallization without legacy case/artifact coupling.
test_steps: Apply the new migrations to an empty database and a realistic substrate snapshot.
Run repository smoke checks for person lookup, source/evidence linkage, and run ledger writes.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: This replaces generic entity-first plus legacy Stage6 truth storage as the target model. IMPLEMENT-002-A/B/C completed with active-baseline schema for person identity, source/evidence storage, run ledgers, and durable-object metadata without reusing frozen Stage6 truth tables as canonical storage.

tasks.description: Refit the preserved ingestion, archive, media, and session pipeline so Stage 5 outputs normalized source, evidence, and provenance records aligned to the new person-first schema while preserving rebuildability.
acceptance_criteria: Realtime ingest, archive import, media enrichment, and session slicing write source/evidence/provenance records keyed to the new data model.
Stage 5 remains corpus-first and does not materialize legacy Stage6 artifacts or durable person conclusions.
A full rerun from raw inputs can deterministically rebuild the cleaned substrate.
test_steps: Run realtime, archive, and media smokes on seeded data and verify new source/evidence/provenance rows.
Replay the same input twice and confirm deterministic rebuild behavior.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: This keeps the reusable ingest core but narrows Stage 5 to the PRD substrate boundary. IMPLEMENT-003-A/B/C now write realtime, archive, session, and media-derived source/evidence/provenance rows into the new substrate model, and IMPLEMENT-003-D added deterministic replay verification via --stage5-smoke to catch count/identity drift and non-substrate writes.

tasks.description: Implement the new control-plane pass protocol and normalization layer: explicit input envelope, stable result statuses, typed candidate outputs, truth-layer separation, and storage-safe normalization.
acceptance_criteria: Pass envelopes carry run type, target object, scope, source refs, truth summary, conflicts, unknowns, and schema version.
Normalization separates fact, inference, hypothesis, and conflict and prevents raw model prose from writing directly into durable state.
Passes can end in result_ready, need_more_data, need_operator_clarification, or blocked_invalid_input with persisted audit records.
test_steps: Exercise normal, need-more-data, clarification, and invalid-input runs and verify stored run and normalization records.
Feed unsafe or malformed outputs and confirm they are downgraded or blocked.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: This is the main governance seam between reasoning and durable state. IMPLEMENT-004-A/B/C now provide versioned pass envelopes, guarded typed normalization, and persisted audit records for model_pass_runs plus normalization_runs across result_ready, need_more_data, need_operator_clarification, and blocked_invalid_input outcomes, with CLI smokes covering the contract and audit paths.

tasks.description: Build PRD Stage 6 bootstrap on the prepared corpus: operator-centered graph initialization, tracked-person attachment, linked-person discovery, candidate identity handling, bootstrap slices, contradiction pools, and ambiguity pools without durable promotion.
acceptance_criteria: A bootstrap run can execute for one tracked person or corpus scope and persist non-durable graph-init and ambiguity outputs.
Linked person, candidate identity, and mention states are explicitly differentiated in stored bootstrap results.
Repeated runs are idempotent and do not duplicate graph edges or bootstrap artifacts.
test_steps: Seed a realistic corpus for one tracked person and run bootstrap twice, comparing counts and object identities.
Verify that bootstrap outputs remain non-durable and are not presented as reviewed dossiers or profiles.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: This is a new bootstrap stage, not a repair of the old Stage6 engine. IMPLEMENT-005-A/B/C now provide non-durable operator-root graph initialization, tracked-person attachment, linked-person/candidate/mention discovery, and bootstrap ambiguity_pool, contradiction_pool, and bootstrap_slice outputs with idempotent rerun behavior verified through --stage6-bootstrap-smoke.

tasks.description: Build PRD Stage 7 durable formation for dossiers, profiles, pair dynamics, events, timeline episodes, story arcs, and reviewable durable knowledge objects from normalized candidates and bootstrap outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Durable object families exist with evidence refs, freshness, stability, confidence, coverage, contradiction markers, and promotion state.
Profile versus dossier is explicit, pair dynamics is first-class, and timeline uses episodes and story arcs instead of legacy period summaries as the end state.
Formation logic remains separate from promotion governance and from later crystallizer reruns.
test_steps: Run durable formation on a seeded bootstrap scope and verify stored person, dossier, pair-dynamics, event, and timeline outputs.
Rerun the same scope and confirm revision behavior instead of duplicate durable rows.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: This is the first real product-grade knowledge layer required by the PRD. IMPLEMENT-006-A/B/C now materialize dossier/profile, pair-dynamics, and timeline/event/story-arc durable families with shared metadata and evidence links, and IMPLEMENT-006-D adds consistent revision and idempotent rerun behavior across those families so identical reruns reuse stable durable identities while changed reruns append family-specific revisions instead of duplicating rows.

tasks.description: Build PRD Stage 8 crystallization and scoped recompute: trigger queue, dependency model, targeted reruns after new data, edits, deletes, and operator answers, plus promotion and clarification governance.
acceptance_criteria: A persistent recompute queue exists with dedupe, retry, and smallest-sufficient-scope execution.
Promotion and clarification gates operate on the new truth-layer model instead of legacy Stage6 case/artifact state.
Clarification answers, edits, deletes, and high-signal data create targeted reruns instead of global rebuilds.
test_steps: Apply clarification, edit, delete, and high-signal triggers and verify only affected scopes are rerun.
Exercise promotion-approved, promotion-blocked, and conflict-heavy candidates and verify stored outcomes.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Completed via IMPLEMENT-007-A/B/C: Stage8 now has a persistent deduping recompute queue with retry and smallest-sufficient-scope execution, trigger ingestion for clarification answers/edits/deletes/high-signal updates, and truth-layer promotion + clarification gates that update durable_object_metadata promotion_state/truth_layer outcomes with auditable gate metadata.

tasks.description: Implement new operator surfaces on the clean model: person-root workspace, dossier/profile/pair/timeline views, clarification and review queues, and clean programmatic reads for those same objects.
acceptance_criteria: Navigation starts from tracked person, linked person, or relevant graph context instead of legacy Stage6 case/chat shell.
Operator views expose truth layers, freshness, contradiction pressure, and review state clearly for each durable object family.
Primary operator workflow no longer depends on legacy Stage6 queue/case/artifact pages.
test_steps: Start the web runtime locally and walk through person-root dossier, profile, pair-dynamics, timeline, clarification, and review flows.
Verify that canonical, proposal, operator-supplied, and obsolete states are visually distinct and consistent across pages.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Moved to a separate web/tg interface PRD track after core knowledge/control implementation. Not part of the current backend-core execution path.

tasks.description: Implement observability, defect taxonomy, runtime control states, and budget governance for the new pass pipeline, normalization, promotion, clarification, and crystallization flows.
acceptance_criteria: Metrics or logs exist for pass families, normalization outcomes, promotion blocks, contradiction load, clarification load, recompute depth, model spend, and runtime control states.
Defect classes and escalation rules cover ingestion, data, identity, model, normalization, control-plane, cost, and semantic-drift failures.
Runtime states such as normal, degraded, review_only, budget_protected, promotion_blocked, and safe_mode are visible and enforceable.
test_steps: Inject cost, normalization, contradiction, and missing-context failure scenarios and verify recorded defects and control-state transitions.
Confirm queryable metrics expose run family duration, promotion-blocked counts, recompute queue depth, and budget by scope.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Completed through IMPLEMENT-009-A/B/C: pipeline metrics/rollups, runtime defect taxonomy persistence with escalation rules, and enforceable runtime control states (normal, degraded, review_only, budget_protected, promotion_blocked, safe_mode) now govern Stage8 execution and promotion behavior.

tasks.description: Backfill and validate the clean model from preserved substrate data, update runbooks for core knowledge/control pilot rollout and rollback, and run the pre-rollout audit before any cutover beyond pilot scopes.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot rebuild can run from substrate-only data with resumable checkpoints and clear restart behavior.
Runbooks describe core knowledge/control pilot rollout, rebuild, and rollback for the clean architecture instead of legacy Stage6/web/tgbot flows.
A pre-rollout review note or approval exists and rollout is not approved until blocking findings are resolved.
test_steps: Restore a representative substrate snapshot, run partial rebuild, interrupt it, resume it, and verify stable counts and object identities.
Walk through the updated runbooks and confirm commands, service names, and rollback steps match the current codebase.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln and the core pilot verification flow.
tasks.notes: Completed through IMPLEMENT-010-A/B/C after all dependencies were satisfied: resumable pilot rebuild/backfill flow is in place, runbooks were updated for clean-architecture pilot rollout/rollback, and the pre-rollout audit gate is recorded in execution backlog state.

tasks.description: Add retrieval-loop and pass-budget guardrails so Stage 6/7/8 runs cannot enter unbounded need_more_data cycles or silently burn cost on one scope.
acceptance_criteria: Each pass family has explicit iteration, token, and cost budgets per scope or run.
Excessive need_more_data loops terminate into explicit blocked or escalated outcomes instead of continuing indefinitely.
Loop guard events are persisted and visible to runtime control-state and defect handling.
test_steps: Simulate repeated need_more_data responses and verify the run is terminated at configured limits with an auditable terminal status.
Verify normal successful runs stay below the same guardrails without false positives.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Completed by adding auditable per-pass budget envelopes plus enforcement that converts exhausted need_more_data loops into terminal operator-clarification outcomes and runtime defect signals for Stage 6/7/8 control-plane handling.

tasks.description: Implement partial-graph continuation under unresolved clarifications so blocked identity or context branches do not freeze unrelated Stage 7/8 progress.
acceptance_criteria: Clarification-blocked branches are stored explicitly and scoped to affected graph segments.
Unrelated dossier, pair-dynamics, and timeline branches can continue on partial graph state where safe.
Blocked branches surface their dependency clearly without stalling unaffected work.
test_steps: Create a clarification-blocked identity branch and verify unaffected branches continue durable formation and crystallization.
Verify affected branches remain blocked and auditable until clarification arrives.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Completed through IMPLEMENT-012-A/B: clarification_branch_states now persist branch-scoped unresolved clarification blocks, and Stage8 recompute execution consults those branch states so only the affected target family remains clarification-blocked while unaffected dossier/profile, pair-dynamics, or timeline work in the same scope can continue safely.

tasks.description: Implement identity-resolution safety controls: reversible merge history, merge confidence tiers, and bounded invalidation or recompute after merge or split corrections.
acceptance_criteria: Identity merges and splits are reversible with preserved before and after state.
Weak or ambiguous merges cannot silently promote poisoned durable knowledge.
Corrections trigger bounded invalidation or recompute on affected graph branches instead of forcing global reruns by default.
test_steps: Approve and then reverse a merge and verify affected graph and durable knowledge return to a consistent state.
Inject a weak merge candidate and verify promotion is blocked or explicitly review-gated.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Completed through IMPLEMENT-013-A/B: identity merges now persist reversible before/after snapshots with confidence-tier review gates, and applied or reversed corrections trigger bounded durable-object invalidation plus scoped Stage8 dossier/profile, pair-dynamics, and timeline recompute plans for only the affected persons rather than defaulting to global reruns.

tasks.description: Implement a canonical dossier-field registry and field-key normalization policy so extensible dossier output cannot drift into hundreds of duplicate semantic keys.
acceptance_criteria: Durable dossier fields use canonical field families with alias mapping for near-duplicate keys.
New field families remain proposal-only until normalized and approved.
Normalization prevents semantically duplicate keys from fragmenting durable reads.
test_steps: Submit overlapping field keys such as favorite_food and gastronomic_preferences and verify canonical mapping or blocked proposal behavior.
Verify durable reads aggregate through canonical keys rather than raw model labels.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Addresses dossier schema drift caused by open-ended LLM field generation. Completed by adding a canonical registry plus alias catalog and enforcing Stage7 dossier normalizer gating so approved aliases collapse into canonical durable fields while unapproved families remain proposal-only.

tasks.description: Implement freshness and decay policy for durable knowledge so local episodes, situational states, and recency-sensitive behavior do not stay wrongly authoritative forever.
acceptance_criteria: Stable traits, situational states, and local episodes have distinct freshness and decay semantics.
Read models can down-rank or obsolete stale durable conclusions when newer contradictory evidence arrives.
Stage 8 refresh logic uses recency-aware weighting instead of blindly preferring old durable knowledge.
test_steps: Create an old situational state and newer contradictory evidence and verify the stale state is downgraded or expired appropriately.
Verify durable stable traits remain more persistent than local episodes under the same decay logic.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Addresses stale guidance risk from outdated durable knowledge in Stage 8. Completed by persisting explicit decay classes/policies, applying policy-aware freshness scoring in Stage7 refresh paths, and using recency-aware Stage8 gating to downgrade stale contradictory situational/local conclusions more aggressively than stable traits.

tasks.description: Implement backfill resource-safety controls: per-scope locking, bounded concurrency, deadlock-aware retries, and resumable checkpointing for large archive rebuilds.
acceptance_criteria: Large rebuilds use bounded concurrency and per-scope locking to avoid cross-scope write contention.
Database deadlocks and transient write conflicts are retried with bounded backoff and durable checkpoints.
Partial multi-hour runs can resume safely without duplicating or corrupting durable objects.
test_steps: Run a concurrent backfill scenario over multiple scopes and verify bounded worker concurrency with stable checkpoint progress.
Inject a deadlock or transient conflict and verify retry, backoff, and recovery behavior without data loss.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: Completed through IMPLEMENT-016-A/B: bounded per-scope backfill leasing with durable checkpoints now includes deadlock/transient conflict classification, bounded retry backoff, and persisted recovery telemetry (retry/deadlock/transient counters plus recovery timestamps) with smoke coverage for retry and recovery behavior.

tasks.description: Execute the safe-deletion batch after runtime-critical remediation: remove orphan directories, orphan TgAssistant.Web residue, clearly unused placeholders, and safe-to-delete stale repository surface already proven to have no active consumers.
acceptance_criteria: Orphan filesystem residue is removed from the repo without touching active runtime seams.
The orphan TgAssistant.Web tree and other empty placeholder files no longer remain in the mainline source tree.
Safe-to-delete stale repository surface is removed only where caller analysis already proved no active usage.
test_steps: Verify the target paths no longer exist or are removed from the solution/runtime composition.
Search the codebase for remaining references to the deleted placeholder and stale repository contracts.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: First post-remediation cleanup batch: only delete candidates already classified as safe after the runtime-critical remediation run.

tasks.description: Isolate the remaining legacy Stage6 bot/autocase/network cluster and trim stale config or DI residue so legacy diagnostics remain explicit and cannot drift back into the active baseline by ambient wiring.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy Stage6 bot/autocase/network code is moved behind a tighter legacy boundary or clearly marked as non-baseline diagnostics only.
Legacy config bindings and DI registrations for BotChat, Web, and Stage6AutoCaseGeneration are removed or gated by explicit legacy-only switches.
No active startup path or config default suggests those clusters are normal runtime behavior.
test_steps: Search Host startup and settings registration for BotChat, Web, and Stage6AutoCaseGeneration and confirm only explicit legacy diagnostics remain.
Inspect the isolated Stage6 bot/autocase/network surface and verify it no longer leaks into active composition by default.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: This batch is isolation-first, not broad deletion: keep risky Stage6Bootstrap/Stage7/Stage8 seams untouched while tightening obvious legacy runtime residue.

tasks.description: Reclassify the DB boundary after remediation: separate truly active mappings and repositories from frozen legacy ones, then cut or retire the stale repo surface that no longer belongs in the active baseline.
acceptance_criteria: The active versus frozen DB boundary is explicit in DbContext and repository registrations.
Stale repositories are either removed, isolated, or justified with clear active callers and storage purpose.
No schema reduction is attempted until active-path versus frozen-path ownership is fully documented.
test_steps: Trace callers for candidate stale repositories and confirm the chosen keep/isolate/delete treatment is code-backed.
Inspect TgAssistantDbContext and related registrations to verify active and frozen mappings are no longer ambiguous.
Run dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.
tasks.notes: High-risk cleanup batch. Focus on boundary truth and stale repository surface first; only then consider any future schema reduction.

tasks.description: Rebuild the docs authority chain after cleanup: restore an explicit planning index, merge or archive pre-PRD option notes, and ensure active markdown reflects the current baseline and gateway-extension positioning.
acceptance_criteria: Active docs authority is explicit and points to the current PRD, gateway PRD, and current backlog rather than stale or missing indices.
Pre-PRD option notes and residual planning docs are archived, merged, or clearly downgraded out of the active authority chain.
README and planning surfaces describe the current runtime and cleanup state without legacy Stage6/web/bot baseline claims.
test_steps: Open the active docs authority entrypoints and verify they point to the current baseline documents only.
Search for references to missing or deprecated planning docs and confirm active markdown no longer depends on them.
Run any repo markdown or link validation used for docs changes, if available.
tasks.notes: Final post-cleanup docs pass. This should happen after code and DB-boundary cleanup so the authority chain reflects the real project state.
